_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, scorecard-refine-local cron)
_meta.note: FL local batch — Bovo (Hialeah, currency fix: RESIGNED Apr 27 2025) + Diamond (Jacksonville D13) + Freeman (Jacksonville At-Large Grp 1, running for FL House) + Kennedy (Melbourne D6/Vice Mayor) + Hanley (Melbourne D5). Evidence-based LOCAL-tier finalization. Rosters web-verified June 2026. Confidence = evidence_local where positions scored; roster_verified for Hanley (all rubric cells null after thorough sweep). Every TRUE/FALSE cites a primary or reputable source; null where no evidence (no party-default inference). Applies the same Kirk Lane discipline used in prior Melbourne batch — NO votes whose stated rationale was 'no local ties' (not anti-Christian) leave christian_liberty null, consistent with Bassett/Smith/LaRusso treatment.

set.office: Mayor of Hialeah, Florida (former · resigned April 27, 2025 to join The Southern Group, a D.C. lobbying firm)

profile.religion: Roman Catholic (parishioner; specific parish not extractable from the official Hialeah bio PDF — binary not OCR-readable in this sweep)
profile.background: Esteban 'Steve' Bovo Jr., b. June 12, 1962 in Queens, NY to Esteban Bovo Carás (2506 Brigade / Bay of Pigs veteran). AA Business Administration, Miami-Dade Community College (1983); BS Political Science, Florida International University (1987). Pre-politics: marketing/advertising executive, Hialeah Park Race Track. Career: Hialeah City Council 1998-2008; Florida House District 110 (2008-2011, resigned to run for county); Miami-Dade County Commissioner District 13 (2011-2020, served as Chair); ran for Miami-Dade Mayor 2020 (won R primary, lost general); elected Mayor of Hialeah Nov 2, 2021; RESIGNED April 27, 2025 to join The Southern Group lobbying firm in D.C., following wife Viviana Bovo (longtime Marco Rubio staffer, now State Department). Five children: Oscar, Bianca, Esteban, Sofia, Alessandro. Interim Mayor Jacqueline Garcia-Roves (City Council President) sworn in April 2025 — Hialeah's first female mayor; special election Nov 2025.

esteban-bovo-jr.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] CURRENCY FIX: Bovo is NOT the sitting Mayor of Hialeah — he resigned April 27, 2025 to join The Southern Group, a D.C. lobbying firm. Status moved to former; candidacy_status not_running. Interim mayor Jacqueline Garcia-Roves now holds the seat. HARD evidence (cited) on retained positions: (a) biblical_marriage q2 TRUE — as Miami-Dade Commissioner Dec 2, 2014, Bovo voted NO on the transgender-inclusive Human Rights Ordinance (passed 8-3), publicly saying 'There are cases of the deviants who will take advantage of this' and conditioning support on a bathroom-use carve-out; direct rejection of gender-identity-as-protected-class framing. (b) biblical_marriage q4 FALSE — Bovo CO-SPONSORED the Miami-Dade LGBTQ Advisory Board ordinance in 2019, publicly thanked by Equality Florida; that is direct affirmative promotion of LGBTQ+ public policy. The record is genuinely mixed (q2 anti / q4 pro) and is scored that way per the no-overread, no-inference rule. (c) public_justice q0 TRUE — 2020 mayoral campaign explicitly opposed defunding/restructuring police: 'Rethinking everything about our police only feeds into what I think is a very, very loud, minority'; police-union support based on no-defund record; affirmative anti-defund advocacy clearing the FXBG high bar. (d) border_immigration q2 TRUE — as Hialeah Mayor pushed Resolution 2025-073 (March 2025) authorizing 287(g) MOA with ICE, explicitly framed as avoiding sanctuary-city designation under Tallahassee pressure. NOT scored on border_immigration q1 (mandatory deportation): Bovo explicitly HEDGED — 'If the question is, are we gonna pull people and ask for their immigration papers? That's not what we're trained to do, that's not what we're authorized to do' — runs against the q1 high bar. NOT scored on refuse_state_overreach despite 2020-21 anti-mask-mandate statements ('I'm not in favor of mandates on anything'): he was ALIGNED with DeSantis, not refusing state directives — DeSantis was also anti-mandate, so this isn't 'local resistance to state overreach' on the rubric reading. NOT scored on christian_liberty q3 despite routine Hialeah council invocation practice: invocations are institutional, not Bovo-led action. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life (the Hialeah pro-life-clinic-vandalism 'hate crime' framing is sympathetic but case-specific, not a personhood/abolition statement), school-choice / parental rights, election integrity, or self-defense — those cells null. DEVIATION ALERT: R out-of-line on biblical_marriage q4 (2019 LGBTQ Advisory Board co-sponsorship).

2.note: As Miami-Dade Commissioner Dec 2, 2014, voted NO on the transgender-inclusive Human Rights Ordinance (passed 8-3); on the dais: 'There are cases of the deviants who will take advantage of this'; conditioned support on a bathroom-use carve-out — direct rejection of gender-identity-as-protected-class.

4.note: Co-sponsored Miami-Dade County LGBTQ Advisory Board ordinance in 2019; publicly thanked by Equality Florida — direct affirmative promotion of LGBTQ+ public policy, out of line with the q4 rubric position.

0.note: 2020 Miami-Dade mayoral campaign publicly opposed defunding/restructuring of police: 'Rethinking everything about our police only feeds into what I think is a very, very loud, minority'; police-union support based on his no-defund record — affirmative anti-defund advocacy clearing the FXBG high bar.

2.note: As Mayor pushed Resolution 2025-073 (March 2025) authorizing a 287(g) MOA between Hialeah PD and ICE — explicitly framed as avoiding sanctuary-city designation under Tallahassee pressure; affirmative anti-sanctuary action and federal-preemption-aligned posture.

set.office: City Council Member, District 13 (Beaches), City of Jacksonville (sitting · term-limited, ends June 30, 2027 · widely reported as a likely 2027 mayoral candidate)

profile.background: Rory Diamond — Jacksonville City Council District 13 (Atlantic Beach, Jacksonville Beach, Neptune Beach, Mayport, Intracoastal West). Elected May 2019; re-elected 2023 (defeated write-in only). Former litigator at international firms; former Special Assistant U.S. Attorney. Captain, U.S. Army / Florida National Guard JAG — deployed to the Middle East with the 40th Infantry Division in 2023 as Chief of Military Justice; returned to council duty Feb 2024. Former CEO (2014-2023) of K9s For Warriors (national veterans / service-dog nonprofit headquartered near Jacksonville); co-authored the federal PAWS Act (Puppies Assisting Wounded Servicemembers) signed in 2021 with then-Rep. Ron DeSantis. Wife reportedly Lauren Blocker. Term-limited from D13 after 2027; widely reported considering 2027 Jacksonville mayoral run.

rory-diamond.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: sitting District 13 council member (Beaches), term to June 30, 2027 (term-limited); widely reported as likely 2027 Jacksonville mayoral candidate. HARD evidence (cited): (a) sanctity_of_life q0 TRUE — publicly stated 'abortion to me and my family and to people who believe as I do is murder' when opposing reimbursement of city-employee abortion-travel; sponsored FY25-26 'Big Beautiful Budget Amendments' banning city funds for 'abortion and abortion-related services as defined by Florida law' — affirmative personhood-grade conviction statement. (b) biblical_marriage q4 FALSE — voted YES on June 9, 2020 (15-4) to RE-CODIFY Jacksonville's Human Rights Ordinance protecting sexual orientation AND gender identity in employment, housing, and public accommodation (the same ordinance later used against Queen of Angels Catholic bookstore until a 2023 religious-exemption settlement). His 2025 anti-DEI budget amendments do not retroactively reverse the 2020 vote — q4 FALSE stands. (c) public_justice q0 TRUE — Jacksonville FOP endorsement in 2019; on the dais opposing 2020 defund amendments to JSO: 'To pretend that taking half the funding from our JSO is not defunding our police is just completely irrational... They're always one second away from death... I find this effort, this amendment, absolutely disgusting'; sustained K9s For Warriors veterans/LE-adjacent CEO bio (2014-2023); JAG officer bio — multiple independent affirmative-advocacy signals clearing the FXBG high bar. (d) border_immigration q2 TRUE — sponsored 2025 Jacksonville ordinance barring city grant recipients from using city money to support services for immigrants without permanent legal status; on the record: 'Jacksonville will never be a sanctuary city' and accused Mayor Deegan of 'making Jacksonville a sanctuary city under our noses' via her veto — direct anti-sanctuary, federal-preemption-aligned action. (e) self_defense q1 TRUE — publicly defended Florida's 2004 state firearms preemption statute against Jacksonville's de facto carry-permit registry (JSO carry logbooks): 'I absolutely support the law... it sure looks like it to me' (re: the AG's 2026 lawsuit) — direct opposition to a city-level gun registry under the q1 rubric scope (red-flag / mag limits / AWB / registries). NOT scored on sanctity_of_life q1 (abolition / personhood-grade legal regime): his policy posture operates within Florida's existing legal framework, not personhood/abolition. NOT scored on economic_stewardship q2 despite 1%-cap balanced-budget local legislation: q2 is narrowly about deficit spending / federal BBA, and the 1%-cap is local fiscal restraint — per the FXBG discipline that excludes 'generic low-tax claims' from q2, leave null. NOT scored on refuse_state_overreach q2 despite anti-DEI budget amendments: anti-DEI cuts are local action against local programs, not refusal of state overreach. NOT scored on self_defense q0: Florida already has constitutional carry by state law (HB 543, 2023) — Diamond defended state preemption against city registries but no direct permitless-carry statement located. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on biblical_marriage q0/q2, family/child-sovereignty, christian_liberty cells, election integrity, public_justice q1-q4 — null. Religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND (specific church not located, though his pro-life quote 'people who believe as I do' implies religiously informed conviction).

0.note: Publicly stated 'abortion to me and my family and to people who believe as I do is murder' when opposing reimbursement for city-employee abortion travel; sponsored FY25-26 'Big Beautiful Budget Amendments' banning city funds for 'abortion and abortion-related services as defined by Florida law' — affirmative personhood-grade conviction statement.

4.note: Voted YES on June 9, 2020 (15-4) to re-codify Jacksonville's Human Rights Ordinance protecting sexual orientation AND gender identity in employment, housing, and public accommodation — direct affirmative codification of LGBTQ+ identity in public policy. 2025 anti-DEI work does not retroactively reverse the 2020 vote.

0.note: Jacksonville FOP endorsement in 2019; on the dais opposing 2020 JSO defund amendments: 'To pretend that taking half the funding from our JSO is not defunding our police is just completely irrational... They're always one second away from death... I find this effort, this amendment, absolutely disgusting'; sustained K9s For Warriors CEO bio (2014-2023); JAG officer — multiple affirmative-advocacy signals clearing the FXBG high bar.

2.note: Sponsored 2025 Jacksonville ordinance barring city grant recipients from using city money to support services for immigrants without permanent legal status; on the record: 'Jacksonville will never be a sanctuary city' and accused Mayor Deegan of 'making Jacksonville a sanctuary city under our noses' via her veto — affirmative anti-sanctuary, federal-preemption-aligned action.

1.note: Publicly defended Florida's 2004 firearms preemption statute against Jacksonville's de facto carry-permit registry (JSO carry logbooks); on the record re: AG's 2026 lawsuit: 'I absolutely support the law... it sure looks like it to me' — direct opposition to a city-level gun registry under q1 rubric scope.

set.office: City Council Member, At-Large Group 1, City of Jacksonville (sitting · resigning Nov 2, 2026 to run for Florida House District 12 · special election to fill seat Aug 18, 2026)

profile.background: Terrance Freeman — Jacksonville City Council At-Large Group 1, R; age ~51. Career: Executive Council Assistant to former Council Member Aaron Bowman starting 2015; subsequently Senior Project Manager / Senior Program Manager of Apprenticeship Programs at Miller Electric Company. Appointed by Gov. Rick Scott to Council District 10 in 2018; elected At-Large Group 1 in 2019; re-elected 2023 with 65% (100,297 votes — highest of any candidate on ballot). Council Vice President 2021; Council President 2022-2023. Chairs Rules Committee; serves on Finance Committee. Married, father of five. Bachelor's and master's degrees in physical education / sports psychology. Father was an Army veteran and Baptist minister; mother a career social worker. Submitted resignation May 28, 2026 to run for Florida House District 12; formally vacates council seat Nov 2, 2026; HD-12 Republican primary Aug 18, 2026 (currently unopposed); special election to fill council seat Aug 18, 2026.

terrance-freeman.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: sitting Jacksonville At-Large Group 1 council member; Council President 2022-2023; second elected term; SUBMITTED RESIGNATION May 28, 2026, formally vacates Nov 2, 2026 to run for Florida House District 12 (HD-12 R primary Aug 18, 2026, currently unopposed; special election Aug 18, 2026). candidacy_status: running_higher_office. HARD evidence (cited): (a) biblical_marriage q2 FALSE + q4 FALSE — voted YES on June 9, 2020 (15-4) on Ordinance 2020-0244 to RE-CODIFY Jacksonville's Human Rights Ordinance protecting LGBT INCLUDING gender identity (after FL appellate court invalidated prior version on technical grounds); did NOT join the four-member dissent (White / Becton / Ferraro / Newby). Direct affirmative vote to codify gender-identity-as-protected-class (q2 FALSE) AND to advance LGBTQ+ identity in city public policy (q4 FALSE). No subsequent repeal effort during his Council Presidency. (b) public_justice q0 TRUE — campaign-site priority: 'Supporting Our First Responders to Fight Crime and Keep Jacksonville Safe'; Council Presidency 'ensured full funding' / 'record funding' for JSO and Fire & Rescue; Sheriff T.K. Waters HD-12 endorsement: 'Terrance Freeman has always backed the men and women in uniform'; FOP Lodge 5-30 (President Randy Reaves) endorsement: 'Terrance Freeman has always stood with the men and women who put on the badge'; X post defending JSO against 'Far-Left' attacks: 'T.K. Waters is an amazing leader who Jacksonville is lucky to have as our Sheriff' — multiple affirmative-advocacy signals + LE-leadership endorsements clearing the FXBG high bar. (c) border_immigration q2 TRUE — voted YES on Ordinance 2025-0138 (passed 6/12/25), prohibiting city tax dollars going to illegal immigrants and establishing city-level immigration enforcement coordination; on the record: 'This bill was not filed to hurt people... it was filed to ensure that government fulfills its top priority, which is to keep its citizens safe'; campaign-site claim 'helped lead the charge to prohibit tax dollars from going to illegal immigrants' — affirmative anti-sanctuary / federal-preemption-aligned action. NOT scored on border_immigration q1 (mandatory deportation): ordinance is enforcement-coordination + funding-restriction, not a mandatory-deportation regime; per discipline, q1 null. NOT scored on sanctity_of_life q1: Freeman RECUSED from the FY25-26 budget vote (which included Diamond's pro-life/anti-DEI/anti-illegal-immigrant amendments) due to Miller Electric / city contract conflict — recusal is not a position; q1 null. NOT scored on economic_stewardship q2 despite Council-President millage reduction: 1/8-mill cut is local fiscal restraint, not federal BBA endorsement; per FXBG discipline q2 null. NOT scored on refuse_state_overreach: Freeman INVITED state DOGE oversight of city finances — the OPPOSITE of refusing state overreach; null. NOT scored on christian_liberty q3 despite 1/10/24 personal introduction of Hope Baptist Church Pastor Tim Nelson for council invocation: routine institutional invocation, not affirmative defense of prayer-in-public-bodies against challenge; null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life q0, biblical_marriage q0, family-child-sovereignty, christian_liberty q0/q2/q4, election integrity, public_justice q1-q4, or self_defense — null. DEVIATION ALERT: R out-of-line on biblical_marriage q2 + q4 (2020 HRO YES vote, including gender-identity protections) — significant culture-war evidence against the rubric direction. Possible name-conflation with same-named 'Terrance Freeman' principal/baseball coach at Eagle's View Academy — career details DO NOT match; do not import that bio. Religion: father Baptist minister noted in background; no personal denominational identity in cited record — religion field left null.

2.note: Voted YES on June 9, 2020 (15-4) on Ordinance 2020-0244 to re-codify Jacksonville's Human Rights Ordinance protecting LGBT INCLUDING gender identity (after FL appellate court invalidated prior version on technical grounds); did NOT join the four-member dissent — direct affirmative codification of gender-identity-as-protected-class.

4.note: Same June 9, 2020 vote on Ordinance 2020-0244 codified SOGI protections in city policy. No subsequent public Freeman effort to repeal HRO during his Council Presidency or otherwise.

0.note: Campaign-site priority of 'Supporting Our First Responders to Fight Crime and Keep Jacksonville Safe'; Council Presidency ensured full / record funding for JSO and Fire & Rescue; Sheriff T.K. Waters HD-12 endorsement: 'Terrance Freeman has always backed the men and women in uniform'; FOP Lodge 5-30 (President Randy Reaves) endorsement: 'Terrance Freeman has always stood with the men and women who put on the badge'; X post defending JSO — multiple affirmative-advocacy signals clearing the FXBG high bar.

2.note: Voted YES on Ordinance 2025-0138 (passed 6/12/25), prohibiting city tax dollars from going to illegal immigrants and establishing city-level immigration-enforcement coordination; on the record: 'This bill was not filed to hurt people... it was filed to ensure that government fulfills its top priority, which is to keep its citizens safe'; campaign-site: 'helped lead the charge to prohibit tax dollars from going to illegal immigrants' — affirmative anti-sanctuary, federal-preemption-aligned action.

set.office: City Council, District 6 (Vice Mayor as of Nov 2025), City of Melbourne, Florida (sitting · term to Nov 2026; first elected Nov 6, 2018; re-elected Nov 8, 2022)

profile.background: Julie Kennedy (formerly Julie Sanders through 2024 — name change appears to be marriage-related; same person — sitting D6 council member, b. ~1971, longtime significant other Darin, a retired USAF veteran / NASA Artemis systems engineer). Age ~55. Grew up in New York; relocated to Brevard County in 1992 after college. Bachelor's in psychology and elementary education, College of Saint Elizabeth (NJ); Master's in educational leadership, Nova Southeastern University. 30-year career educator with Brevard Public Schools; currently Assistant Principal / building administrator at Brevard Adult and Community Education. Two children (Kyle, Emily). Treasurer, Space Coast League of Cities. Active with Boy Scouts of America and Boys & Girls Club of Brevard. Elected D6 Nov 6, 2018; re-elected Nov 8, 2022 (term ends Nov 2026, requires re-election fall 2026). Appointed Vice Mayor in 2021; selected Vice Mayor again Nov 2025 in a 4-3 council vote succeeding outgoing Vice Mayor David Neuman.

julie-kennedy.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: sitting D6 council member; appointed Vice Mayor Nov 2025 in 4-3 council vote (succeeded outgoing Vice Mayor David Neuman); first elected Nov 6, 2018; re-elected Nov 8, 2022 with current term to Nov 2026. NAME CHANGE: appears in 2018-2024 records as 'Julie Sanders' (campaign site, Space Coast Daily, Space Coast Rocket); from 2025 forward as 'Julie Kennedy' — same person. HARD evidence (cited): (a) public_justice q0 TRUE — 2022 re-election endorsed by Police Benevolent Association (PBA) AND Association of International Federation of Fire Fighters (AIFF) — two organized first-responder labor endorsements covering both LE and Fire; campaign stated platform identifies her as 'proud supporter of First Responders' and emphasizes LE technology funding; LE labor endorsements are HARD single-issue equivalents per the skill's social-media protocol — clears the FXBG affirmative-advocacy bar. Also endorsed by Brevard Clerk of Courts Rachael Sadoff and former Brevard Clerk Scott Ellis. NOT scored on christian_liberty q4 for her Sept 24, 2025 NO vote on Mayor Alfrey's 'Charlie Kirk Lane' rename: consistent with the discipline applied to Bassett / Smith / LaRusso in the prior Melbourne batch — Kirk Lane is a memorial naming of a Christian-affiliated public figure, not a Christian symbol or prayer policy, and available reporting attributes the rejecting side's rationale to 'no local ties' rather than ideological opposition; null per the no-overread rule. NOT scored on border_immigration q1/q2 despite Melbourne International Airport PD's 287(g) Task Force Model MOA dated 4/25/2025: the Airport Authority is an independent body governing the airport PD — not a Kennedy individual roll call; null. NOT scored on economic_stewardship q2 despite AYE vote on FY25-26 budget that raised operating millage to 7.0112 mills: q2 is narrowly about deficit spending / federal BBA, not millage votes; per FXBG discipline null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage, family-child-sovereignty (career Brevard Public Schools administrator background creates STRUCTURAL alignment with public-school system but yields zero sourced policy statements either way), christian_liberty q0/q2/q3, election integrity, public_justice q1-q4, refuse_state_overreach, self_defense — null. Religion / church: NO EVIDENCE FOUND. Partisan registration: officially non-partisan race; no public party registration confirmed in this pass.

0.note: 2022 re-election endorsed by Police Benevolent Association (PBA) AND Association of International Federation of Fire Fighters (AIFF) — two organized first-responder labor endorsements (LE + Fire); also endorsed by Brevard Clerk of Courts Rachael Sadoff and former Clerk Scott Ellis; campaign platform identifies her as 'proud supporter of First Responders' and emphasizes LE technology funding — single-issue LE labor endorsements clear the FXBG affirmative-advocacy high bar.

set.office: City Council, District 5, City of Melbourne, Florida (sitting · term to Nov 2028; first elected Nov 3, 2020 succeeding Paul Alfrey who vacated D5 to become Mayor; re-elected Nov 5, 2024 with ~65%)

profile.background: Mariann 'Mimi' Hanley — sitting D5 council member, City of Melbourne, FL. Long-time Melbourne resident. Licensed CPA, operating Mimi Hanley CPA (700 N Wickham Rd, Melbourne); small woman-owned business owner; licensed pilot. Stated priorities: maintain/improve current services, restore the Indian River Lagoon, change codes to prohibit clear-cutting for new development, increase environmental protection. Melbourne council seats are 4-year at-large elected, officially NON-PARTISAN — but Hanley is listed by name in the 'Elected Officials' section of the Brevard County Democratic Executive Committee (brevarddems.org) and BrevardDems publicly congratulated her on Facebook, providing HARD evidence of D affiliation. Elected Nov 3, 2020 succeeding Paul Alfrey (who vacated D5 to run for Mayor); re-elected Nov 5, 2024 with ~65%. Current term runs to Nov 2028.

mimi-hanley.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: sitting D5 council member; first elected Nov 3, 2020 (succeeded Paul Alfrey, who vacated D5 to become Mayor); re-elected Nov 5, 2024 with ~65%; current term to Nov 2028. Partisan registration: officially non-partisan race, but Hanley is listed by name in the 'Elected Officials' section of brevarddems.org (Brevard County Democratic Executive Committee) and BrevardDems publicly congratulated her on Facebook — HARD party evidence; party set to D. NOT scored on christian_liberty q4 for her Sept 24, 2025 NO vote on Mayor Alfrey's 'Charlie Kirk Lane' rename — applying the same discipline used for Bassett / Smith / LaRusso / Kennedy in the prior Melbourne batch: Kirk Lane is a memorial naming of a Christian-affiliated public figure, not a Christian symbol or prayer policy, and Hanley's stated on-the-record rationale was 'Charlie Kirk didn't even know where Brevard County was. He never even heard of Melbourne. I feel we have other people in this community who could be honored.' That is a 'no local ties / pick someone with Melbourne roots' rationale, NOT an anti-Christian-display statement; per the no-overread rule, null. Logged here in the audit trail because the dismissive tone is on the record. NOT scored on economic_stewardship q2 despite AYE on FY25-26 budget raising operating millage to 7.0112 mills: q2 is narrowly about deficit/federal-BBA, not millage; per FXBG discipline null. NOT scored on border_immigration q1/q2 despite Melbourne International Airport PD's 287(g) MOA dated 4/25/2025: the Airport Authority is an independent body governing the airport PD, not a Hanley individual roll call; null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life, biblical marriage (no Hanley-specific statement on Pride / drag / Pride flags / trans-affirming policy), family-child-sovereignty, christian_liberty q0/q2/q3, election integrity, AFFIRMATIVE pro-LE advocacy (no Sheriff Ivey endorsement, no FOP endorsement, no anti-defund resolution found — routine MPD budget votes do not clear the FXBG high bar), refuse_state_overreach, or self_defense — null. Religion / church: NO EVIDENCE FOUND. DEVIATION ALERT: BrevardDems public listing as one of 'their elected officials' is the strongest single signal of left-of-center alignment but per rubric rules is documented context, not scoring evidence.
